description: State-owned national operator, holds main network concession until 2034. Runs Intercity, Sprinter, and international services.
Largest regional operator. Runs services in Friesland, Groningen, Drenthe, Gelderland, Limburg, and Overijssel.
Regional brand by Keolis. Operates Overijssel lines: Enschede-Almelo, Zwolle-Emmen, Zutphen-Oldenzaal.
Blauwnet-branded services operated under Arriva concession in Overijssel.
Blauwnet-branded services operated under Keolis concession in Overijssel.
Unified regional brand used by NS, Qbuzz, and others in Randstad area (Zuid-Holland, Utrecht).
R-net services operated by Qbuzz, including Merwede-Lingelijn (Dordrecht-Geldermalsen).
Alternate Qbuzz R-net service code.
R-net services operated by NS in the Randstad region.
International arm of NS. Operates Intercity Direct, Thalys/Eurostar, and cross-border IC services.
German national railway. Runs cross-border regional services into the Netherlands (e.g. Arnhem-Dusseldorf).
DB Regio cross-border services including Regionalbahn Westfalen and Euregiobahn to Dutch stations.
German subsidiary of Keolis. Operates Hengelo-Bielefeld and other cross-border services.
Belgian national railway. Runs stopping services Roosendaal-Antwerpen and Maastricht-Liege.
Regional brand in Gelderland and Overijssel, operated by Arriva.
RRReis services under Arriva concession.
RRReis services under Keolis concession.
Alternate Arriva RRReis service code.
Regional operator in Limburg, runs Maastricht-Kerkrade and Heerlen-Aachen services.
Regional brand in Arnhem-Nijmegen area, operated under Arriva concession.
Special and charter train services.
Special and charter train services.
Night train startup (est. 2023). Runs Brussels-Amsterdam-Berlin-Prague and Paris-Berlin overnight services.
Low-cost open-access startup (launched 2026). Amsterdam-Berlin and Amsterdam-Hamburg from EUR 10.
French-owned operator. Runs Blauwnet services in Overijssel and Valleilijn (Amersfoort-Ede).
Alternate code for Eurobahn cross-border services.
